  "content": "
<<<EXTERNAL_UNTRUSTED_CONTENT id="cab1c9527ffc546c">>>
Source: Web Search
---
**Simplified cloud-based AI agent platforms include no-code/low-code SaaS options like Gumloop, Lindy, Siit, Relay.app, and CrewAI, which prioritize ease of use for quick automation without deep coding.** These compete with more developer-oriented or enterprise tools but stand out for simplicity, drag-and-drop interfaces, and minimal setup.[1][2][4][6]

### Key Competitors and Comparison
The table below summarizes top simplified cloud-based platforms, focusing on **key features**, **pricing** (starting tiers as of 2026), and **target audience**, drawn from recent comparisons. Enterprise-heavy platforms (e.g., Kore.ai, Vellum) are excluded for their complexity.

| Platform     | **Key Features**                          | **Pricing (Starting)** | **Target Audience**                  |
|--------------|-------------------------------------------|-------------------------|--------------------------------------|
| **Gumloop** | Drag-and-drop builder, prebuilt workflows for data scraping/enrichment, Zapier/API integrations, no-code focus.[1][4][6] | Not specified (SaaS model)[1] | Marketing/ops teams needing quick automations.[6] |
| **Lindy**   | No-code workflows, context-aware adaptation, text-based inbox automation.[2][3] | $49.99/mo[2][3]       | No-code teams, personal productivity, small businesses.[2][3] |
| **Siit**    | Pre-built agents for IT/HR/Finance, Slack/Teams integration, no building required.[2] | $23/admin/mo[2]       | Small business IT/HR/Finance teams.[2] |
| **Relay.app**| Visual builder, app integrations, workflow automation for agencies.[4] | Free plan, then $38/mo[4] | Agencies, service providers, customer success.[4] |
| **CrewAI**  | Role-based multi-agent orchestration, YAML config, code/no-code flexibility.[1][2] | Free to Enterprise[2] | Growing teams starting simple, scaling to custom.[1][2] |
| **Dify**    | Open-source RAG/multi-model support, rapid prototyping.[1] | Cloud SaaS (pricing not detailed)[1] | Teams wanting accessible self-hosting or cloud prototyping.[1] |

### Additional Insights
- **Common Strengths**: These platforms emphasize **no-code speed** (e.g., Gumloop's drag-and-drop[1][6]), pre-built templates, and SaaS deployment for fast ROI without infrastructure management.[1][2][4]
- **Differentiation**: Simpler tools like Siit/Lindy target non-technical users with ready agents[2], while CrewAI adds multi-agent scalability for mid-sized teams.[1][2] All are cloud/SaaS-primary, unlike self-hosted options (e.g., n8n).[3]
- **Limitations**: Pricing can scale with usage; some lack deep enterprise governance (e.g., no built-in RBAC like StackAI).[1] For small businesses, free tiers (Relay.app, CrewAI) lower entry barriers.[2][4]
<<<END_EXTERNAL_UNTRUSTED_CONTENT id="cab1c9527ffc546c">>>",

{
  "query": "Gumloop features pricing target audience",
  "provider": "perplexity",
  "model": "perplexity/sonar-pro",
  "tookMs": 6857,
  "externalContent": {
    "untrusted": true,
    "source": "web_search",
    "provider": "perplexity",
    "wrapped": true
  },
  "content": "
<<<EXTERNAL_UNTRUSTED_CONTENT id="5e5583512026157d">>>
Source: Web Search
---
**Gumloop** is an AI-native workflow automation platform with a credit-based pricing model featuring Free, Pro (starting at $37/month), and Enterprise (custom) plans, designed primarily for teams, power users, agencies, and businesses building complex AI automations.[1][3][6][7]

### Key Features
Gumloop enables no-code building of AI agents and workflows via drag-and-drop interfaces, integrating AI models without add-on fees.[2][6]
- **Core tools**: Unlimited agents/workflows (paid plans), workflow interfaces, webhooks, triggers (1 on Free, unlimited on Pro+).[6]
- **Collaboration**: Shared ownership, organization templates, shared credentials/API keys, unlimited seats/teams on Pro+.[1][3][6]
- **Performance**: Concurrent runs (2 on Free, 5 on Pro, custom on Enterprise), agent interactions (5/25/custom).[3][5][6]
- **Enterprise extras**: Role-based access control (RBAC), SCIM/SAML, audit logs, VPC, custom data retention, workflow queuing.[3][5][6]
- **Analytics/Support**: Team usage analytics, unified billing (Pro+); email/Slack support, Gumloop University (paid).[6]
Free plan allows testing with built-in AI nodes and basic limits.[2][3]

### Pricing
Pricing uses **credits** for automation/AI processing (e.g., workflows, batch jobs, AI calls); higher usage requires upgrades or add-ons. Annual billing offers ~20% discount; outdated sources mention "Solo/Team" plans (now merged into Pro).[1][7]
| Plan      | Price          | Credits/Mo     | Key Limits/Features |
|-----------|----------------|----------------|---------------------|
| **Free** | $0            | 5,000         | 1 seat, 1 trigger, 2 concurrent runs, 5 agent interactions; unlimited flows.[3][5][6][7] |
| **Pro**  | Starts at **$37/mo** (20k+ credits; scales up e.g., to 55k for $97/mo) | 20,000+      | Unlimited seats/teams/triggers, 5 runs/25 interactions, BYO API keys, analytics.[1][3][5][6] |
| **Enterprise** | Custom     | Custom        | All Pro + advanced security/scalability (RBAC, SSO, VPC).[3][5][6] |

Costs can rise with heavy AI/batch use; Pro upgraded from prior Solo/Team for more credits/collaboration at same/lower prices.[1][4][7]

### Target Audience
Gumloop targets **teams with AI ambitions**, power users, agencies, small-to-medium businesses, and enterprises needing collaborative AI workflows—effective for non-technical users via intuitive interface but scales for complex automations.[1][2][7][8][9]
- Ideal for: Team collaboration, AI-heavy batch processing (not simple triggers).[1][7]
- Less suited for: High-volume single tasks like cheap email enrichment.[7]
Free tier suits beginners/testing; Pro/Enterprise for scaling teams.[3][8]
<<<END_EXTERNAL_UNTRUSTED_CONTENT id="5e5583512026157d">>>",
  "citations": [
    "https://www.gumloop.com/blog/simplifying-gumloop-pricing",
    "https://www.bannerbear.com/blog/4-minute-gumloop-review-features-use-cases-ideal-users/",
    "https://www.marketermilk.com/blog/gumloop-review",
    "https://www.lindy.ai/blog/gumloop",
    "https://metaflow.life/blog/gumloop-reviews",
    "https://www.gumloop.com/pricing",
    "https://prospeo.io/s/gumloop-pricing-reviews-pros-and-cons",
    "https://till-freitag.com/blog/gumloop-ai-automation-review-en",
    "https://cybernews.com/ai-tools/gumloop-ai-review/",
    "https://www.datacamp.com/tutorial/gumloop-tutorial"
  ]
}

args.query: Lindy AI features pricing target audience

stdout:
{
  "query": "Lindy AI features pricing target audience",
  "provider": "perplexity",
  "model": "perplexity/sonar-pro",
  "tookMs": 7246,
  "externalContent": {
    "untrusted": true,
    "source": "web_search",
    "provider": "perplexity",
    "wrapped": true
  },
  "content": "\n<<<EXTERNAL_UNTRUSTED_CONTENT id=\"d85a861fa5b7cddf\">>>\nSource: Web Search\n---\n### Features\nLindy AI is an **AI assistant platform** for automating business processes, including inbox management, meeting recording and note-taking, calendar handling, email drafting in your voice, and hundreds of integrations (e.g., HubSpot, Salesforce, Gmail, Slack).[5][9][2] Key capabilities include a drag-and-drop workflow builder for non-coders, custom AI agents for tasks like online research, CRM updates, and meeting prep/follow-up, plus advanced options like computer use, iMessage integration 24/7, and voice calls (starting at $0.19/minute via GPT-4o).[5][2][3][7] It uses a credit-based system where simple tasks cost 1-3 credits, complex ones 10+, with models like GPT-4o or Claude 3.5 consuming more; top-ups available at $10/1,000 credits.[2][3][4] The AI learns your style over time and supports multichannel workflows, though voice features are add-ons and less optimized for high-volume calling.[2][3][5]\n\n### Pricing\nPricing varies across sources, with the official site listing **Pro at $99.99/month** (3x usage vs. Plus, 3 inboxes, computer use) and **Max at $199.99/month** (7x usage, 5 inboxes, more computer use); all include core features like inbox management and integrations, but third-party reviews cite lower/conflicting tiers (e.g., Free: $0/400 credits ~40 tasks; Pro: $29.99-$59.99/month/3,000 credits; Business: $199.99/20,000 credits; Enterprise: custom).[5][1][2][3][4] Credit consumption is pay-as-you-go (e.g., $0.01-$0.10/task), non-refundable, with annual discounts possible and team add-ons at ~$19.99/user; free tier/tests available for 400-500 credits.[2][4][7][9] Reviews note unpredictability due to variable credit burn, making it cost-effective for low-volume but risky for high-use.[3][4]\n\n| Plan (Official) | Monthly Price | Key Limits |\n|-----------------|---------------|------------|\n| Pro            | $99.99       | 3x usage, 3 inboxes, computer use[5] |\n| Max            | $199.99      | 7x usage, 5 inboxes, more computer use[5] |\n\nThird-party tiers often differ (e.g., Pro $29.99-$49.99, Business $199.99), so verify on-site.[1][2][4]\n\n### Target Audience\n**Teams and individuals** needing cross-tool automation for internal workflows (e.g., email/CRM/meetings), power users/solo operators for AI agents, and businesses for scalable agents without coding.[1][3][4][9] Best for marketers, sales teams automating prospecting/research, and non-technical users via drag-and-drop; less ideal for high-volume customer-facing voice or unpredictable heavy use due to credits.[2][3][7] Enterprise suits teams requiring custom security/SSO/HIPAA.[4]\n<<<END_EXTERNAL_UNTRUSTED_CONTENT id=\"d85a861fa5b7cddf\">>>",
  "citations": [
    "https://www.eesel.ai/blog/lindy-ai",
    "https://blog.dograh.com/decoding-lindy-ai-pricing-and-plans-in-2025/",
    "https://www.dialora.ai/blog/lindy-ai-reviews",
    "https://prospeo.io/s/lindy-pricing-reviews-pros-and-cons",
    "https://www.lindy.ai/pricing",
    "https://www.salesforge.ai/directory/sales-tools/lindy",
    "https://www.lindy.ai/blog/generative-ai-marketing-strategy",
    "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=-9mX4e5zqHQ",
    "https://www.lindy.ai"
  ]
}

  "content": "\n<<<EXTERNAL_UNTRUSTED_CONTENT id=\"29323a4c5b59363e\">>>\nSource: Web Search\n---\n**Siit is an AI-powered employee service platform for internal IT and HR support, targeting IT/HR teams in organizations needing efficient request management.** Its pricing uses per-admin monthly subscriptions (billed annually), with tiers from $23 to $89 per admin, scaling for teams and enterprises[3][2][7].\n\n### Key Features\nSiit focuses on modern ticketing, self-service, and AI-driven automation integrated with tools like Slack, Teams, Email, Notion, and Confluence[3][5][2]:\n- **Essentials ($23/admin/mo)**: Conversational request management, knowledge base integrations, approvals, service catalog, email/chat support[3].\n- **Standard ($45/admin/mo)**: Adds automated workflows, asset/SaaS management, HRIS/MDM/ticketing integrations, service forms, reporting/analytics, Slack Connect support[3].\n- **Pro ($89/admin/mo)**: Includes AI agents for end-to-end workflows, **AI Assist**, **AI Triage**, monitoring, custom reports/analytics, advanced support[3].\n- **Custom (pricing on request)**: Enterprise integrations, advanced workflows, dedicated support for large-scale needs[3].\n\nPricing is admin-based (those managing requests), with unlimited requesters and multi-team workspaces; no hidden fees[3].\n\n### Target Audience\nSiit serves **IT and HR teams** handling employee requests, from small teams to enterprises, emphasizing scalability for mid-market and large organizations via usage-aligned models[2][3][5]. It suits stable teams avoiding per-user scaling costs, with AI features for high-volume support[2].\n<<<END_EXTERNAL_UNTRUSTED_CONTENT id=\"29323a4c5b59363e\">>>",

{
  "query": "Relay.app features pricing target audience",

    "source": "web_search",

    "wrapped": true
  },
  "content": "
<<<EXTERNAL_UNTRUSTED_CONTENT id="8efdf082cb36f006">>>
Source: Web Search
---
### Features
Relay.app is a workflow automation platform with built-in AI capabilities, supporting automations across apps like Gmail, Notion, and HubSpot. Key features include access to multiple AI models (GPT, Claude, Gemini), **shared workflows and connections** for teams, unlimited automations/playbooks in paid plans, Human-in-the-Loop controls, and adjustable limits on **AI credits** (for AI actions) and **workflow steps** (executions).[1][2][3][5][8]

All plans provide core automation tools; differences lie in usage limits, users, and collaboration.[3][2]

### Pricing
Relay.app uses a tiered, usage-based model combining seat pricing with monthly **AI credits** and **workflow steps**. Pricing varies slightly across sources (likely due to updates or billing options), but official details emphasize annual billing discounts. Free tier available for all; additional AI credits purchasable.[1][2][3]

| Plan        | Monthly (Annual Billing) | Users | AI Credits/Mo | Steps/Mo | Notes |
|-------------|---------------------------|-------|---------------|----------|-------|
| **Free**   | $0                       | 1-2  | 500          | 200     | Full features for testing/light use[1][3][6] |
| **Professional/Starter** | $19 (or $18-$38)     | 1-2  | 5,000-10,000 | 750-10,000 | Solo users; scales with usage[1][2][3][4][5][6] |
| **Team**   | $69 (or $27-$98)         | 10 (or 3-20) | 5,000     | 2,000   | Collaboration focus; ~$6.90-$9/user[1][2][3][4][5][7] |
| **Enterprise** | Custom                | Custom | Custom     | Custom  | Compliance, support, training[1][3][4] |

*Conflicts noted:* Sources differ on exact user counts, steps/credits, and monthly rates (e.g., Team at $27 vs. $69), with official site confirming $19/$69 starts and usage scaling.[3] Month-to-month higher; no setup fees.[1][2]

### Target Audience
- **Free/Professional:** Solo operators, small businesses, individuals testing automations or with moderate needs.[1][2][3][6]
- **Team:** Collaborative groups like marketing/sales teams (up to 10-20 users) needing shared workflows.[1][3]
- **Enterprise:** Large orgs (50+ users) with compliance (SOC2/GDPR), custom integrations, or high volume.[1][3]
Best for non-technical users valuing AI access, transparency over per-action billing, vs. volume-based competitors like Zapier.[1][5][9]
<<<END_EXTERNAL_UNTRUSTED_CONTENT id="8efdf082cb36f006">>>",

}
